---
title: "CB3 Equity Report — Environmental Conditions Maps"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: cb3
---

## Purpose

This notebook reads the clean Environmental Conditions tract table and maps four
representative dimensions:

1. Rodent inspections (confirmed activity): `rodent_active_inspections`
2. Street and sidewalk cleanliness: `avg_acceptable_streets_pct`, `avg_acceptable_sidewalks_pct`
3. Heat Vulnerability Index (by ZCTA), combined with the EJ Area overlay and
   individual street trees colored by health status
4. EJ Area designation: `ej_area_flag` (shown as a hatched overlay on every map)

Every metric is presented as a bubble layer sized by magnitude over selectable
demographic choropleth base layers. Rodent inspections are point events with
native building coordinates, so their bubbles plot at each building's own
address. Street and sidewalk cleanliness are reported at the DSNY cleaning
section level, not census tract, so their bubbles sit at each section's
centroid instead. The Heat Vulnerability Index is reported by ZCTA (ZIP), so
its bubbles sit at each ZCTA's centroid, with the ZCTA boundary drawn as an
outline overlay and individual living street trees (2015 TreesCount census)
plotted at their own coordinates, colored by health status. Map HTML files
are written to `maps/environment/`.

```{python}
# Import packages for tract data, geometry, classification, and interactive maps.
from pathlib import Path
import sys
import yaml

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd

# Ensure the reusable scripts package imports whether this QMD is rendered from
# the project root or from scripts/.
for candidate in [Path.cwd(), *Path.cwd().parents]:
    if (candidate / "src" / "map_utils.py").exists():
        sys.path.insert(0, str(candidate))
        break

from src.cb3_utils import add_polygon_centroids
from src.map_utils import (
    add_categorical_point_layer,
    add_zero_value_legend,
    build_metric_bubble_map,
    find_project_dir,
)
```

```{python}
# Define project inputs and output locations. Resolve the project root so the
# notebook works whether it is rendered from the root folder or from scripts/.
PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "environment_tract.csv"
SECTIONS_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "environment_sections.csv"
BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "cb3_tract_baseline_profile_clean.csv"
)
RODENT_INSPECTION_POINTS_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "environment_rodent_inspection_points.csv"
)
HEAT_VULNERABILITY_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "environment_heat_vulnerability.csv"
TREE_POINTS_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "environment_tree_points.csv"
TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "raw" / "Geography" / "2020_Census_Tracts_20260505.geojson"
)
SECTIONS_GEOMETRY_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "raw" / "Geography" / "DSNY_Sections_20260630.geojson"
)
ZCTA_GEOMETRY_PATH = (
    PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "raw" / "Geography" / "ZIP_Code_Tabulation_Areas_20260708.geojson"
)
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "environment"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

with open(PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml") as f:
    ALL_METRICS = yaml.safe_load(f)
```

## Prepare tract geometry and metrics

```{python}
# Read the clean metrics with GEOID preserved as an 11-character string.
metrics = pd.read_csv(CLEAN_DATA_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})

# Read the 2020 tract polygons, normalize GEOID, and retain only the 31 CB3 rows.
tract_geometry = gpd.read_file(TRACT_GEOMETRY_PATH)
tract_geometry["GEOID"] = tract_geometry["geoid"].astype("string").str.zfill(11)
tract_geometry = tract_geometry[["GEOID", "geometry"]]

# Join the metrics to polygons and verify a complete one-to-one match.
tracts = tract_geometry.merge(
    metrics,
    on="GEOID",
    how="inner",
    validate="one_to_one",
)
tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(tracts, geometry="geometry", crs=tract_geometry.crs)

assert len(tracts) == 31
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique
assert set(tracts["GEOID"]) == set(metrics["GEOID"])

# Read tract-level demographics and join them to the same 31 tract polygons.
baseline_profile = pd.read_csv(BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH, dtype={"geoid": "string"})
baseline_profile["GEOID"] = baseline_profile["geoid"].str.zfill(11)

DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS = [
    "GEOID",
    "median_household_income",
    "age_0_to_19_share",
    "age_20_to_64_share",
    "age_65_plus_share",
    "white_non_hispanic_share",
    "black_non_hispanic_share",
    "asian_non_hispanic_share",
    "hispanic_share",
    "poverty_rate",
    "lep_household_share",
]

tracts = tracts.merge(
    baseline_profile[DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="one_to_one",
)

# Read building-level rodent inspection points (native lat/lon, not collapsed
# to a tract centroid) and attach tract/NTA labels for tooltips.
rodent_insp_points = pd.read_csv(RODENT_INSPECTION_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
rodent_insp_points = rodent_insp_points.merge(
    tracts[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="many_to_one",
)

# Read individual living street tree points (native lat/lon) and attach
# tract/NTA labels for tooltips.
tree_points = pd.read_csv(TREE_POINTS_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"})
tree_points = tree_points.merge(
    tracts[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="many_to_one",
)
```

```{python}
# Build METRIC_SPECS and DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS from metric_metadata.yml.
# ej_area_flag is excluded here — it is rendered as a dedicated overlay, not a
# bubble layer, because it is binary and the concept doc calls for a hatched overlay.
def _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=False):
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label":     meta["label"],
        "unit":      meta["unit"],
        "palette":   meta["palette"],
    }
    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta["filename"]
    for key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"):
        if key in meta:
            spec[key] = meta[key]
    return spec

# Tract-level environment metrics (excludes ej_area_flag and section/ZCTA-level metrics).
METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if (
        meta["domain"] == "environment"
        and var != "ej_area_flag"
        and "filename" in meta
        and not meta.get("geography", "").startswith(("DSNY", "ZCTA"))
    )
}

# Section-level sanitation metrics (DSNY cleaning sections, not census tracts).
SECTION_METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta.get("geography", "").startswith("DSNY") and "filename" in meta
}

# ZCTA-level metrics (ZIP Code Tabulation Areas, not census tracts).
HVI_METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta.get("geography", "").startswith("ZCTA") and "filename" in meta
}

# senior_poverty_rate is registered as a demographic metric for other domains'
# notebooks (e.g. seniors), but is not featured as a layer here.
EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS = {"senior_poverty_rate"}

DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "demographics" and var not in EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS
}

for metric in METRIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
for metric in DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
```

```{python}
def add_ej_overlay(map_object, show=True):
    """Add EJ-designated tracts as a dashed-border checkbox overlay.

    True SVG hatching is not available in Folium without third-party plugins,
    so EJ tracts are rendered with a semi-transparent fill and a dashed border
    to distinguish them from the underlying choropleth/bubble layers.
    """
    ej_tracts = tracts[tracts["ej_area_flag"].eq(1)][["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name", "geometry"]].copy()
    folium.GeoJson(
        data=ej_tracts.to_json(),
        name="EJ Area (2024)",
        overlay=True,
        show=show,
        style_function=lambda _: {
            "fillColor": "#d95f02",
            "color": "#d95f02",
            "weight": 2,
            "dashArray": "5 4",
            "fillOpacity": 0.20,
        },
        highlight_function=lambda _: {
            "weight": 3,
            "fillOpacity": 0.35,
        },
        tooltip=folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
            fields=["tract_label", "nta_name"],
            aliases=["Census tract", "NTA"],
            sticky=False,
        ),
    ).add_to(map_object)
    add_zero_value_legend(
        map_object,
        "#d95f02",
        "Designated EJ Area (MOCEJ 2024)",
    )


# Thin, environment-domain-specific wrappers around the generic map builders
# in src/map_utils.py, so call sites below don't have to repeat tracts/
# METRIC_SPECS/DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS/MAP_OUTPUT_DIR on every call. The EJ overlay
# is added on top of every bubble map since it has no equivalent in the
# generic builders.
def create_single_metric_map(metric, **kwargs):
    # The EJ overlay is passed as build_metric_bubble_map's extra_layers hook
    # so it's added before that function's internal LayerControl, not after —
    # a layer added after LayerControl renders in a later script tag than the
    # one that references it, which throws a JS ReferenceError and silently
    # breaks the whole control.
    spec = METRIC_SPECS[metric]
    return build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts, METRIC_SPECS, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS, metric,
        title=f"CB3 Environmental Conditions: {spec['dimension']}",
        output_path=MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"],
        extra_layers=lambda m: add_ej_overlay(m, show=False),
        **kwargs,
    )


# Load cleaning section geometry and averages for the section-level maps.
# The DSNY sections geojson uses the 'section' column (MN031–MN034), which
# matches the 'section' column in environment_sections.csv.
sections_geometry = gpd.read_file(SECTIONS_GEOMETRY_PATH)
sections_geometry = sections_geometry[sections_geometry["district"] == "MN03"][
    ["section", "geometry"]
].copy()
sections_geometry = sections_geometry.to_crs("EPSG:4326")

sections_metrics = pd.read_csv(SECTIONS_DATA_PATH)
assert len(sections_metrics) == 4, f"Expected 4 CB3 cleaning sections, got {len(sections_metrics)}"

# Attach each section's polygon centroid (sections have no native building-level
# coordinates), so cleanliness scores can be bubbled the same way as every
# other metric map instead of shown as a section-level choropleth.
section_points = add_polygon_centroids(
    sections_metrics, sections_geometry, "section",
    lat_col="section_centroid_latitude", lon_col="section_centroid_longitude",
)


def add_section_outline_overlay(map_object, show=True):
    """Add DSNY cleaning section boundaries as an unfilled checkbox overlay.

    Bubbles sit at each section's centroid, so the outline gives viewers the
    actual section boundary the bubble is standing in for.
    """
    layer_data = sections_geometry[["section", "geometry"]]
    folium.GeoJson(
        data=layer_data.to_json(),
        name="DSNY cleaning sections",
        overlay=True,
        show=show,
        style_function=lambda _: {
            "fillOpacity": 0,
            "color": "#3825a5",
            "weight": 3,
        },
        tooltip=folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
            fields=["section"],
            aliases=["Cleaning section"],
            sticky=False,
        ),
    ).add_to(map_object)


def create_section_map(metric):
    def extra_layers(map_object):
        add_ej_overlay(map_object, show=False)
        add_section_outline_overlay(map_object, show=False)

    spec = SECTION_METRIC_SPECS[metric]
    return build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts, SECTION_METRIC_SPECS, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS, metric,
        title=f"CB3 Environmental Conditions: {spec['dimension']}",
        output_path=MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"],
        points=section_points,
        lat_col="section_centroid_latitude",
        lon_col="section_centroid_longitude",
        tooltip_fields=["section"],
        tooltip_aliases=["Cleaning section"],
        extra_layers=extra_layers,
    )


# Load citywide ZCTA geometry and restrict it to the CB3-overlapping ZCTAs
# already selected in environment.py (environment_heat_vulnerability.csv).
zcta_geometry = gpd.read_file(ZCTA_GEOMETRY_PATH)
zcta_geometry["zcta5"] = zcta_geometry["zcta5"].astype(str)
zcta_geometry = zcta_geometry[["zcta5", "geometry"]].to_crs("EPSG:4326")

heat_vulnerability_metrics = pd.read_csv(HEAT_VULNERABILITY_PATH, dtype={"zcta5": "string"})
cb3_zcta_geometry = zcta_geometry[zcta_geometry["zcta5"].isin(heat_vulnerability_metrics["zcta5"])]
assert len(cb3_zcta_geometry) == len(heat_vulnerability_metrics)

# Attach each ZCTA's polygon centroid (HVI has no native building-level
# coordinates), so the HVI score can be bubbled the same way as every other
# metric map instead of shown as a ZCTA-level choropleth.
zcta_points = add_polygon_centroids(
    heat_vulnerability_metrics, cb3_zcta_geometry, "zcta5",
    lat_col="zcta_centroid_latitude", lon_col="zcta_centroid_longitude",
)

# Living street trees are colored by health status rather than sized by
# magnitude, so each color is a fixed marker fill rather than a legend tied
# to bubble size.
TREE_HEALTH_COLORS = {"Good": "#238b45", "Fair": "#fec44f", "Poor": "#d94701"}


def add_zcta_outline_overlay(map_object, show=True):
    """Add CB3-overlapping ZCTA (ZIP) boundaries as an unfilled checkbox overlay.

    Bubbles sit at each ZCTA's centroid, so the outline gives viewers the
    actual ZIP boundary the bubble is standing in for.
    """
    layer_data = cb3_zcta_geometry[["zcta5", "geometry"]]
    folium.GeoJson(
        data=layer_data.to_json(),
        name="ZCTA (ZIP) boundaries",
        overlay=True,
        show=show,
        style_function=lambda _: {
            "fillOpacity": 0,
            "color": "#08519c",
            "weight": 2,
        },
        tooltip=folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
            fields=["zcta5"],
            aliases=["ZIP Code (ZCTA)"],
            sticky=False,
        ),
    ).add_to(map_object)


def create_heat_vulnerability_map():
    def extra_layers(map_object):
        add_ej_overlay(map_object, show=False)
        add_zcta_outline_overlay(map_object, show=True)
        add_categorical_point_layer(
            map_object,
            tree_points,
            "health",
            TREE_HEALTH_COLORS,
            name="Street trees (health)",
            tooltip_fields=["spc_common", "address"],
            tooltip_aliases=["Species", "Address"],
            show=True,
            # The HVI bubble-size legend occupies the default bottom-right
            # position (up to ~190px tall); offset the tree legend below it
            # so the two don't overlap.
            legend_bottom_offset=230,
        )

    spec = HVI_METRIC_SPECS["heat_vulnerability_index"]
    return build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts, HVI_METRIC_SPECS, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS, "heat_vulnerability_index",
        title="CB3 Environmental Conditions: Heat Vulnerability, EJ Areas & Street Trees",
        output_path=MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"],
        points=zcta_points,
        lat_col="zcta_centroid_latitude",
        lon_col="zcta_centroid_longitude",
        tooltip_fields=["zcta5"],
        tooltip_aliases=["ZIP Code (ZCTA)"],
        extra_layers=extra_layers,
    )
```

## Separate maps


### Rodent inspections

```{python}
# Rodent inspections are reported at the building level, not the tract level,
# so bubbles use each building's own coordinates rather than a tract centroid.
rodent_map = create_single_metric_map(
    "rodent_active_inspections",
    points=rodent_insp_points,
    lat_col="latitude",
    lon_col="longitude",
    tooltip_fields=["address", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["Address", "Census tract"],
)
rodent_map
```

### Street cleanliness (section level)

```{python}
streets_map = create_section_map("avg_acceptable_streets_pct")
streets_map
```

### Sidewalk cleanliness (section level)

```{python}
sidewalks_map = create_section_map("avg_acceptable_sidewalks_pct")
sidewalks_map
```

### Heat vulnerability, EJ Areas & street trees

```{python}
# Heat Vulnerability Index is reported by ZCTA, not census tract, so bubbles
# sit at each ZCTA's centroid with the ZCTA boundary drawn as an outline
# overlay. Living street trees are individual points colored by health status.
heat_vulnerability_map = create_heat_vulnerability_map()
heat_vulnerability_map
```


## Output validation

```{python}
expected_map_files = (
    [MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"] for spec in METRIC_SPECS.values()]
    + [MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"] for spec in SECTION_METRIC_SPECS.values()]
    + [MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"] for spec in HVI_METRIC_SPECS.values()]
)

for map_file in expected_map_files:
    assert map_file.exists()
    assert map_file.stat().st_size > 10_000

print(f"Wrote {len(expected_map_files)} map files to {MAP_OUTPUT_DIR}")
```
